summary: Responsive pass verification across Maestro EGP at mobile (390x844), tablet (768x1024), and desktop (1920x1080). All target routes (/dashboard, /reports, /delivery, /settings, /orders) render content correctly with ZERO horizontal page overflow (documentElement.scrollWidth == window.innerWidth). Order details dialog on mobile fits within viewport (~370.5px in 390px viewport, ~95vw as designed). Desktop unaffected — headers/content render normally.

issues: documentElement.scrollWidth=390 (PASS, no page overflow), but document.body.scrollWidth=1037 — an inner element is wider than viewport and is being clipped by the global overflow-x:clip rule. Page does NOT overflow horizontally, but some dashboard content may be visually cropped at the edge. Worth a code-level review to find the offending wide child (likely a chart/table without min-w-0 or overflow-x-auto wrapper).
document.body.scrollWidth=555 (vs documentElement=390). Same pattern as dashboard — page-level overflow is clipped (PASS) but an inner block is wider than viewport. Minor visual cropping possible.

action_items: Optional: investigate inner elements on /dashboard (body scrollWidth 1037) and /orders (body scrollWidth 555) at mobile widths. Wrap any wide tables/charts with overflow-x-auto + min-w-0 instead of relying solely on global overflow-x:clip, so users can scroll those elements horizontally rather than having content cropped.
critical_code_review_comments: Global overflow-x:clip on html/body below 1024px effectively eliminates page-level horizontal scrolling, which is the desired behavior. However, the dashboard's inner overflow (1037px content in 390px viewport) suggests at least one component does not yet have its own responsive wrapping — relying on clip hides the problem rather than fixes it. Recommend a follow-up audit of Dashboard.js for fixed-width or grid blocks that should be min-w-0 / flex-wrap / overflow-x-auto.
Dialog 95vw rule works correctly — order details dialog measured 370.5px in 390px viewport, no page overflow while opened.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on stated acceptance criteria (no horizontal page overflow + content renders + dialog fits + desktop intact)

seed_data_creation: None. Used pre-existing seeded rejected delivery order #5002 for dialog test.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Iteration 234 verified the broad responsive pass. Numbers measured (documentElement.scrollWidth vs window.innerWidth):
 - MOBILE 390x844: /dashboard 390/390, /reports 390/390, /delivery 390/390, /settings 390/390, /orders 390/390 (all overflow=0).
 - TABLET 768x1024: /dashboard, /reports, /delivery all 768/768 (overflow=0).
 - DESKTOP 1920x1080: /dashboard, /reports, /delivery, /settings all 1920/1920, headers visible.
 - Order details dialog on mobile: width=370.5px, x=9.75 (centered with ~9.75px margins each side ≈ 95vw), fits viewport, page overflow with dialog open = 0.
Note: document.body.scrollWidth on mobile /dashboard=1037 and /orders=555 indicate inner wide elements being clipped by global overflow-x:clip. Acceptance criteria still met. Splash delay observed ~13s post-login and ~8s post-navigation as warned.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found relative to the acceptance criteria. Minor design note in design_issues above.
